steps.title: Apply the shape-color link
steps.content: The passage explains that melanosomes produce pigment and have different shapes depending on which colors they produce. Since scientists found melanosomes in a fossilized bird feather, the hypothesis should use their appearance to infer the bird's color.

steps.title: Avoid unsupported extensions
steps.content: Choice A moves to fossils from other extinct species, which the passage does not establish. Choice C concerns why the feather was preserved, not what color it was. Choice D contradicts the method because it asks about fossils without melanosomes. The supported hypothesis uses the feather's melanosomes to determine color. The answer is B.
